definition: The AI valuation premium is almost entirely a US phenomenon, creating structural ERP differentials: US S&P 500 at 21-22x forward (ERP ~2.5-3.0%) vs Europe Stoxx 600 at 13-14x (ERP ~5-6%), Japan TOPIX at 14-15x (ERP ~4-5%), EM at 11-12x (ERP ~4-6%). US ERP is 200-500bp tighter than every other region. Any AI timeline/magnitude reassessment disproportionately compresses US relative multiples.
evidence: US ERP 200-500bp tighter than every other region
Pair_1 called this a clean trade construction
S&P 500 ex-tech vs MSCI EAFE roughly flat since 2010 — AI as US exceptionalism single point of failure
open_questions: How quickly would US relative multiples compress upon AI reassessment?
Does the regional differential already account for US tech earnings growth differential?
Is ex-US equity outperformance a hedge against AI disappointment or a standalone thesis?
